quant_config.quant_scheme: affine per-group (asymmetric); zero=group min; scale=(max-min)/15; nibble-packed uint8 (N,K/2); scales/zeros fp16 — house _quantize_int4

exempt_tensors.lm_head: TIED to bf16 embedding (P1 resident decision), 0 extra VRAM

resident_vram_note: measure_vram_int4: embedding 1187.0 MiB bf16 + INT4 projections 714.0 MiB + norms 0.47 MiB + tied head 0 = 1901.47 MiB resident (vs P1 tc-bf16 3875.5 MiB). INT4 saves ~1974 MiB of projection weights.

int4_vs_p1bf16_same_engine.note: cached-chain, same engine, both chains drift identically -> isolates INT4 quantization noise (registered spec note)

hard_flip_attribution.note: shared = flip already present in P1 bf16 (drift/port carried through, not INT4-introduced); int4_only = candidate quant-noise flips -> p2_adjudicate fresh-refeeds them

adjudication.method: fresh full-prefill of prompt+decode_tokens[0..s] through INT4 adapter at each INT4-ONLY HARD cached-chain flip; drift if it reverts, real quant-noise if it persists at >0.25 GT margin
adjudication.headline: 92 REAL INT4 quant-noise flip(s) at non-near-tie GT margins (reported, not fixed — quantization is lossy)

ppl_coverage_note: tc-INT4 P2 ppl scored the same first N windows within the 590s cap as P1 tc-bf16 (identical GT tokenization/stride) — so INT4-vs-P1 is a MATCHED delta. P0 HF-bf16 scored the full corpus (299,077 tok); the tc-vs-P0 gap stays coverage-confounded. APA-on vs APA-off within INT4 is also matched.
ppl_8k_wall_note: INT4 w8192 std scored 1 full window (8191 tok, ppl 22.790) before OOM on the 2nd window; P1 bf16 w8192 got 0 windows. INT4 partially lifts the >=8K scoring wall but does NOT clear it — the binding constraint is the fp32 logits (1,L,151936) transient (4.64 GiB @8K), not weights. INT4 apa w8192 OOMs at 0 windows (APA refine buffers add on top) -> APA NEVER ENGAGED, not a valid APA datapoint (June law). >=16K unattempted (above 8K wall).
int4_quant_quality_finding: INT4 is a genuine, measured accuracy loss: (1) fresh-prefill final-position parity 7/11 clean vs P1 bf16 11/11 (4 HARD flips at GT margins up to 4.125 under the P1b port-certification protocol); (2) 92 REAL quant-noise decode flips persist on fresh-refeed; (3) matched-window ppl +5.6-5.8 vs bf16 (22.30 vs 16.47 @2048). Control 10/10 clean -> the INT4 forward is CORRECT where the model agrees; the port is sound, the quantization itself degrades the 1.7B. This is the P2->P3 decision signal: INT4 is MARGINAL, favoring P3 (INT6) per the plan gate.
